title: Logistics & Distribution Supervisor
description:
**Join us to deliver a smoke‑free future**
 We have chosen to do something incredible. We are totally transforming our business and building our future with one clear purpose: to deliver a smoke free future. Swedish Match is an affiliate of PMI and has been a leader in the industry for more than 200 years. In the Nordic region, we are 1,800 employees based in Sweden, Denmark, Finland, and Norway, working across various functions and teams to collaboratively deliver a smoke free Nordic future.
   

  

 To strengthen our team, we are currently seeking a Logistics and Distribution Supervisor, reporting to the Logistics & Distribution Supervisor, Nordics.
   

  

 This position places you in the heart of the business, where operational excellence, teamwork, and a results driven mindset are essential to achieving ambitious daily goals.
   

  

 In our Logistics & Distribution Team we have the mission to handle the entire fulfilment flow to market and the distribution to its customers and consumers in order to fulfill internal and external customers’ expectations and align with all laws and regulations. We contribute to building a unique consumer experience of our multi category product portfolio by guaranteeing the adequate product availability across multi distribution channels demonstrating on contemporary and new solutions to reach outstanding levels.
   

  

**Your “day to day”**
**Responsibilities**
 As a Logistics & Distribution Supervisor, you will lead a team responsible for ensuring efficient, compliant, and customer-focused logistics operations across the Nordic markets. You will play a key role in monitoring end-to-end shipments, managing forward and reverse flows, strengthening partnerships with Logistics Service Providers (LSPs), and driving continuous improvement initiatives aligned with our transformation towards a smoke-free future. You will also:
   

  

* Monitor shipments from factories to Logistics Service Providers (LSPs) for the Nordic markets, ensuring timely and cost-efficient deliveries.
* Oversee customer and B2C forward orders, deliveries, and reverse logistics flows, guaranteeing efficient fulfilment and return processes.
* Manage relationships with LSPs, including contract compliance, performance follow-up, and issue resolution.
* Ensure compliance and operational governance, including audit requirements, internal billing guidelines, stock counts, destructions, and other logistical controls and procedures.
* Collaborate closely with internal and external stakeholders, including Procurement (participation in logistics tenders) and customers, to analyze order patterns, improve service performance, and shape future logistics solutions.
* Drive continuous improvement initiatives across logistics operations, focusing on process optimization, productivity, sustainability, and enhanced customer experience (including return processes).
* Lead and support the logistics operations team, coordinating daily activities, fostering a high‑performing and collaborative environment, and encouraging accountability and continuous learning.


**Who We´re Looking For**
* Minimum 10 years of experience in Supply Chain, preferably within an internationally operating FMCG environment.
* Strong expertise and passion about the Logistics, Sales, and Distribution environment.
* Advanced proficiency in MS Office (experience with SAP or similar ERP systems is an advantage).
* A proactive problem solver who is adaptable and effective in a dynamically changing environment.
* Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively within a team, while also being capable of operating independently.
* An open and improvement driven mindset, with the courage to challenge existing ways of working and drive optimization.
* Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to quickly build effective relationships with internal and external partners.
* High analytical capability, strong learning agility, and a consistent focus on quality and accuracy of work.
* Fluency in English with strong communication skills (Swedish language skills would be a plus).


 All applicants must have the legal right to work in Sweden.
   

  

 Relocation support is not available for this job.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 Our success depends on the people who come to work every single day with a sense of purpose and an appetite for progress. Join PMI and you too can:
   

  

* Seize the freedom to define your future and ours. We’ll empower you to take risks, experiment and explore
* Be part of an inclusive, diverse culture, where everyone’s contribution is respected; collaborate with some of the world’s best people and feel like you belong.
* Enjoy flexibility of hybrid way of working - split your time between working from our office or the comfort of your home, where it works best for you in line with business needs.
* Pursue your ambitions and develop your skills with a global business – our staggering size and scale provides endless opportunities to progress.
* Take pride in delivering our promise to society: to improve the lives of a billion smokers.


**25711**

title: Banking Technology Lead | Financial Services | Stockholm
description:
At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence.
   

  

 We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go.
   

  

 Join EY and help to build a better working world.
   

  

**The opportunity**
 EY is scaling its Nordic Financial Services Technology Consulting practice with a clear ambition to double the business over the next four years. Technology Consulting already represents a significant part of EY’s Nordic Financial Services business, yet we see substantial untapped potential across major Nordic banking clients.
   

  

 This is not a traditional delivery or capability leadership role. As a Banking Account Technology Lead (ATL), you will operate at the intersection of client leadership, commercial ownership, and technology advisory. You will act as a trusted advisor to CIOs and technology leadership teams while shaping EY’s long-term technology position across priority banking accounts.
   

  

 This role offers a unique opportunity to influence the direction of EY’s technology growth in the Nordic banking sector and to work directly with senior client stakeholders on large-scale transformation agendas.
   

  

**Your Key Responsibilities**
* Act as EY’s trusted advisor to CIOs, CTOs, CDOs and broader technology leadership
* Drive technology sales, pipeline development and delivery across priority accounts
* Position EY as a leading transformation partner across design, build and run
* Connect client needs with EY’s full suite of technology capabilities, alliances and solutions
* Increase EY’s share of client technology spend through scalable, repeatable offerings
* Shape account strategy together with account leadership and Global Client Service Partners
* Lead cross‑functional collaboration across Nordic and global EY teams


**Specific focus areas – Banking Technology Lead**
* Lead technology engagement across major Nordic banking clients
* Strengthen EY’s positioning with the largest Nordic banking clients
* Support preferred supplier positioning and large‑scale transformation programmes
* Drive growth across cloud, data, AI and platform modernization agendas


**Skills And Attributes For Success**
 Professional competencies
   

  

* Strong consulting background from a leading firm (Big4, Accenture, Capgemini or similar)
* Deep experience within Banking and Financial Services
* Proven ability to engage and influence technology executives at CIO / CTO level
* Experience leading large‑scale technology transformation programmes
* Strong commercial mindset with a track record of building pipeline and closing deals
* Ability to operate in a Nordic, cross‑border environment


**Ideally, you’ll also have**
* Experience building pipeline and driving complex technology engagements
* Experience scaling consulting offerings and teams
* Experience working within preferred supplier or strategic vendor frameworks


**What We Look For**
**Personal competencies**
* Strong collaboration skills and ability to operate across service lines
* Entrepreneurial drive combined with structured thinking
* High integrity and ability to build trusted relationships
* Growth mindset with passion for technology-driven transformation


**What We Offer You**
 At EY, we’ll develop you with future-focused skills and equip you with world-class experiences. We’ll empower you in a flexible environment and support you in realizing your full potential in a diverse and inclusive culture of globally connected teams.
   

  

 Learn more.
   

  

**Are you ready to shape your future with confidence? Apply today.**
 Please submit your application no later than
 **30th of April 2026** 
 . Selection and interviews will be conducted on an ongoing basis. Please note that a background check will be conducted as part of the recruitment process.
   

  

 At EY, we celebrate diversity and encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds. If you have any questions, require assistance, or need adjustments to the recruitment process due to a disability, please don't hesitate to reach out to us at recruitmentsweden@se.ey.com. We’re here to support you.
   

  

**EY | Building a better working world**
 EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.
   

  

 Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.
   

  

 EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories.

title: Senior Systemtekniker – Drift & Infrastruktur
description:
**Vi söker: Senior Systemtekniker – Drift & Infrastruktur** 




 Linux i terminalen. Windows servrar och Microsoft 365 i molnet. AI i verktygslådan.
 



  





**Om Crossnet** 




 Crossnet AB är en managed service provider (MSP) i Stockholm som hjälper företag att hålla sin IT säker, stabil och modern. Vi hanterar allt från nätverk och servrar till säkerhet, backup och molntjänster och vi gör det med ett tight team som gillar att lösa problem på riktigt, inte bara stänga ärenden.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Vi söker en senior systemtekniker som vill vara med och ladda både serviceteamet och driftteamet. Du blir en teknisk nyckelspelare som rör dig obehindrat mellan Linux-terminalen, Microsoft 365-admin och våra kunders miljöer. Du tar ansvar för drift, felsökning och vidareutveckling av infrastruktur, internt och hos våra viktigaste kunder.
 



  





 Det här är en roll för dig som trivs med variation: ena stunden konfigurerar du webbservrar, nästa stund felsöker du Conditional Access i M365, och efter lunch optimerar du en Remote Desktop-miljö. Du använder AI som ett naturligt verktyg i vardagen och hjälper kollegorna att göra detsamma.
 



  





**Dina huvudsakliga ansvarsområden** 



* Drift och underhåll av våra viktigaste kunders IT-miljöer, inklusive servrar, nätverk och molntjänster
* Förvalta och utveckla Crossnets interna Linux-system — webbservrar, övervakningsplattformar och automationsverktyg
* Administrera Microsoft 365-miljöer med fokus på säkerhet, Intune, Exchange Online och Conditional Access
* Hantera och optimera våra Remote Desktop-miljöer
* Stötta serviceteamet med eskaleringar och teknisk expertis
* Driva effektivisering genom smart användning av AI-verktyg i den dagliga driften



  





**Vi söker dig som har** 



* CompTIA A+-certifiering (krav)
* Djup erfarenhet av Linux-administration — du känner dig hemma i terminalen och kan felsöka, automatisera och konfigurera utan att blinka
* Stark kompetens inom Microsoft 365-administration (Entra ID, Intune, Exchange Online, Defender)
* Erfarenhet av att drifta och felsöka webbservrar (cPanel, Apache/Nginx)
* Praktisk vana vid att använda AI-verktyg för att effektivisera arbetet
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet av IT-drift eller systemadministration, gärna i MSP-miljö
* Flytande svenska och god engelska



  





**Meriterande** 



* Ytterligare certifieringar — Microsoft, Linux (LPIC/RHCSA), Fortinet NSE eller liknande
* Erfarenhet av nätverksövervakning och brandväggar
* Erfarenhet av ledande RMM-plattformar
* Kunskap om enterprise backup- och disaster recovery-lösningar
* Erfarenhet av virtualisering (Xen, VMware, Hyper-V)
* Scriptkunskaper i PowerShell och/eller Bash



  





**Vad vi erbjuder** 



* Ett tight team med hög teknisk nivå där du både lär dig nytt och delar med dig
* En modern, genomtänkt verktygsstack — vi har valt best-of-breed inom varje kategori
* En arbetsplats där AI inte är ett buzzword utan ett verktyg vi faktiskt använder varje dag
* Kontorstid måndag–fredag med en kontorskänsla där man faktiskt gillar att dyka upp
* Möjlighet att påverka både hur vi jobbar och vilka verktyg vi använder



  





**Anställningsform:** 
 Heltid, tillsvidareanställning
 



**Plats:** 
 På plats, Stockholm (mån–fre)
 



  





 Intresserad? Skicka din ansökan till jobb@crossnet.se eller skriv en DM.

title: Detection & Incident Response Engineer
description:
We are looking for an experienced
 **Detection & Incident Response Enginee** 
 r to transform our security operations capability and ways of working. In this role, you will be at the core of detecting, investigating and responding to security threats, while driving automation and improving detection coverage across the organisation.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

* Build and continuously improve security monitoring and alerting capabilities
* Investigate security events and operate the incident response process end-to-end
* Design, implement and tune detection rules and alerts to maximise effectiveness and reduce noise
* Own and maintain incident response procedures and alert playbooks
* Perform threat intelligence activities to enhance detection and response capabilities
* Monitor logs and respond to alerts in a timely and effective manner
* Integrate security tools and data sources into detection and alerting pipelines
* Configure and optimise Security Orchestration, Automation and Response (SOAR) workflows
* Enhance our DLP monitoring
* Contribute to broader security initiatives and tasks as required


**Requirements**
* Strong SOC and security operations experience
* Hands-on experience with SIEM platforms (e.g. Elastic, Microsoft Sentinel)
* Proficiency in KQL
* Solid incident response experience
* Experience with automation and orchestration (SOAR)
* Understanding of SOAP and security integrations


 Experience: 6+ years in a relevant security role
   

  

 Certifications (preferred)
   

  

* SC-200 – Microsoft Security Operations Analyst
* GIAC certifications (e.g. GCIH, GCFA, GNFA, GCIA, GMON, GCDA, GDAT)
* Elastic certifications


 Nice to have
   

  

* Experience participating in blue/purple team exercises
* Experience within iGaming or other regulated industries
* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or similar


 Who you are
   

  

 We are particularly interested in someone who is excited about integrating AI into security operations: leveraging it for smarter detection, faster investigations, and more efficient incident response. We believe you are a self-starter with a strong passion for security, comfortable taking ownership from design to delivery.
   

  

**Life at Relax**
 Are you ready to join a creative, forward-thinking team shaping the future of iGaming? At Relax, we value curiosity, diversity, and the courage to challenge limits. With 350+ colleagues across 10+ locations, we combine stability with constant evolution, driven by passion and teamwork. Relax offers an environment where you can grow, learn, and make real impact. Collaboration is central to how we work, and our success comes from the talent and dedication of our people. If you’re motivated by quality, results, and meaningful connections, you’ll fit right in. No matter where you’re based, you’ll find the same supportive, respectful, and ambitious spirit that defines Relax Gaming.
   

  

**About Relax Gaming**
 Relax Gaming Group was founded in 2010 with the goal of simplifying B2B content delivery for the modern iGaming landscape. With business simplicity and collaboration central to its ethos. Via a quick one-time integration, Relax Gaming now provides access to a roster of 4,000+ casino games to 1,000+ operator brands with a diverse range of proprietary products, including Poker, Bingo, and it's own extensive slot portfolio. High-quality aggregated content from multiple partner studios is provided through its selected Silver Bullet (commercially represented) and Powered By Relax (commercially independent) partners.

title: Temporary Sales associate
description:
**Job Description**
 Core Responsibilities
   

  

  

  
* Ensure a signature experience for every guest that visits our LEGO® stores by greeting every guest in your location, performing all cash register functions and portraying a professional, gracious, friendly and engaging manner at all times
* Provide a clean, safe and visually stimulating environment by performing daily cleaning duties, maintaining store displays and learning and executing LEGO® Brand Retail visual standards
* Safely and efficiently operate the store stockroom as you assist in the receiving of merchandise, maintain the stockroom to LEGO® Brand Retail standards, know and abide by all safety regulations and notify management of potential shortage situations
* Resolve customer issues and report challenging situations to manager
* Customer engagement
* Use of promotional materials, loyalty programs


 This is a temporary position covering from May 4th to August 31st 2026. You will be working in the store, close to the customers. Our customers are in the age 0-99 -) so be ready to learn, play have fun.
   

  

 Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
   

  

**What’s in it for you?**
 Here is what you can expect
   

  

**Family Care Leave** 
 - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.
   

  

**Insurances** 
 – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.
   

  

**Wellbeing** 
 - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.
   

  

**Colleague Discount** 
 – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.
   

  

**Bonus** 
 - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.
   

  

**Workplace** 
 - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.
   

  

 Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.
   

  

 The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.
   

  

 The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.
   

  

 Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.
   

  

 Just imagine building your dream career.
   

  

 Then make it real.
   

  

**Join the LEGO® team today.**

title: Manager, Customer Collaboration (CLD)
description:
**Job Description**
**Are You Ready to Make It Happen at Mondelēz International?**
**Join our Mission to Lead the Future of Snacking. Make It Possible.**
 You will manage the customer collaboration agenda for your market or set of customers. You improve value and service to customers and increase supply chain efficiency while offering accountability and transparency into customer performance via a single point of contact.
   

  

**How You Will Contribute**
 You will:
   

  

* Deliver enhanced service by driving the implementation of customer supply chain improvement initiatives. Establish solid and strategic relationships with customers to achieve long-term cooperation and business partnerships.
* Focus on account or channel-specific initiatives in efficient replenishment and availability while driving the implementation of joint value creation initiatives identified through customer collaboration.
* Drive effective cross-functional communication and collaboration among stakeholders: sales, logistics operations, demand planning, product supply, and external partners.
* Establish Advantage Survey Action Planning in accordance with Mondelez standards.
* Deliver logistics efficiency (Gross Project Productivity targets) by optimizing logistics processes.
* Analyze, report and implement necessary improvements in Route to market area (drop size, case pick, OTIF).


**What You Will Bring**
 A desire to drive your future and accelerate your career and the following experience and knowledge:
   

  

* Experience in Customer Service & Logistics functions with knowledge of customer supply chain management in FMCG or consumer packaged goods company.
* Cross-functional experience (sales, customer service, controlling, demand planning, logistics and warehousing knowledge).
* Strong focus on delivering productivities, service and KPIs.
* Able to analyze data and derive insights into action with a focus on delivering to timelines and KPIs.
* Strong interpersonal, teamwork and communication skills.
* Able to influence, negotiate and apply commercial acumen, strategic thinking with E2E approach.
* Desirable experience from project management.
* SAP, information systems, process design, standard operating procedures.


**What you need to know about this position:**
**Educational Background**
 A university degree in supply chain management, business, economics, or related field is preferred.
   

  

**Job Specific Requirements**
 Experience of Customer Collaboration and Logistics is required.
   

  

**Language**
 Full professional fluency in Swedish and English (written and spoken) is required.
   

  

**Work schedule**
 Fulltime based in Upplands Väsby office.
   

  

**Relocation Support Available?**
 No Relocation support available
   

  

**Business Unit Summary**
 We value our talented employees, and whenever possible strive to help one of our associates grow professionally before recruiting new talent to our open positions. If you think the open position you see is right for you, we encourage you to apply!
   

  

*Our people make all the difference in our succes*
 Mondelēz International is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation or preference, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.
   

  

**Excited to grow your career?**
 We value our talented employees, and whenever possible strive to help one of our associates grow professionally before recruiting new talent to our open positions. If you think the open position you see is right for you, we encourage you to apply!
   

  

**IF YOU REQUIRE SUPPORT TO COMPLETE YOUR APPLICATION OR DURING THE INTERVIEW PROCESS, PLEASE CONTACT THE RECRUITER**
**Job Type**
 Regular
   

  

 Customer Collaboration (CLD)
